title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make with just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. The whole lemon is used in this traditional recipe that takes about 10 minutes to prep and four weeks of fermentation time.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters, without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Pack the whole lemons into a clean jar as tightly as possible, and lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice and add more juice from an extra lemon if needed.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Once opened, store in the fridge indefinitely, rinsing off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes.
